snippets.title: fit (train) and predict (infer) with scikit-learn
snippets.code:
from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression

X_train = [[1.0, 0.2], [0.8, 0.1], [0.3, 0.9], [0.1, 0.7]]
y_train = [1, 0, 1, 0]  # labels required for training

clf = LogisticRegression()
clf.fit(X_train, y_train)       # TRAINING: learn θ from (X, y)

X_new = [[0.9, 0.2], [0.2, 0.8]]  # no labels at inference
y_hat = clf.predict(X_new)      # INFERENCE: ŷ = f_θ(x)
print(y_hat)
snippets.explanation: fit() updates internal coefficients using labeled data; predict() runs the frozen model on new rows without y.

snippets.title: PyTorch train vs eval modes
snippets.code:
import torch
import torch.nn as nn

model = nn.Linear(4, 2)
opt = torch.optim.SGD(model.parameters(), lr=0.1)
loss_fn = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()

X = torch.randn(32, 4)
y = torch.randint(0, 2, (32,))

# TRAINING
model.train()
opt.zero_grad()
logits = model(X)
loss = loss_fn(logits, y)
loss.backward()
opt.step()

# INFERENCE
model.eval()
with torch.no_grad():
    x_new = torch.randn(5, 4)
    preds = model(x_new).argmax(dim=1)
snippets.explanation: train() + backward + optimizer step updates θ; eval() + no_grad() freezes updates for forward-only inference.

snippets.title: Separate training loop from inference function
snippets.explanation: Explicit functions mirror the conceptual split: training consumes y and changes θ; inference only returns ŷ.

snippets.title: Evaluation uses labels but does not train
snippets.code:
from sklearn.metrics import accuracy_score

# After clf.fit(X_train, y_train) — θ is fixed
y_val_pred = clf.predict(X_val)   # inference pass on validation x
acc = accuracy_score(y_val, y_val_pred)  # labels only for metric
# No second fit() — evaluation does not update θ
snippets.explanation: Validation compares predictions to held-out y for metrics, but unlike training it does not call fit or optimizer steps.
